mentions.note: Debt concerns from data center buildout and OpenAI client funding fears. Cramer taking OpenAI concerns off the table but not interested in Oracle's business model at 26x earnings. Would buy lower. Stock hit hard on debt-side worries.
Down 3% in software selloff; enterprise software provider facing market skepticism on AI displacement despite solid fundamentals.
Caller held since $288, now below $50; balance sheet concerns; don't like what they're doing with capital structure; not investable; sell on any bounce.
Most important earnings this week; must show data center build-out going well and making money; OpenAI Abilene site uncertainty knocked hyperscalers/semis; need full skinny from management.
King of data center, fastest growing in space; wait to see how Oracle acts to confirm data center demand strength beyond hyper-scalers.

Q3 beat with 22% revenue growth, $1.79 EPS, RPO +325% YoY; capex-light data center model eases balance-sheet fears. Debt raise $30B completed, no equity dilution near-term. OpenAI stabilized; buyback strong signal.
Solid but others better positioned. Nvidia preferred for AI/compute upside in current environment.
Enterprise software getting crushed in hardware-led rotation; down 3.7%.
Jumped nearly 13% today after straight decline from $345 to $145; data center play has mixed AI disruptability by division.
Down 40% from all-time highs but last quarter very good; should go with Oracle, not a bummer bet.
Explicitly named as data center builder winner by Jensen Wong; integral to infrastructure buildout capital cycle.
Reports Wednesday. Figured out data center buildout business early but outcome still uncertain — 'I don't know if it's working.' Need to see results before committing.
Heavy debt load but 'fine' as a spring-load play on cloud/AI growth; beaten-down valuation offers leverage for those seeking it.
